column_recurrence_target: For h_j=binom(d,j) A_{n+j}, g_j=h_j/h_0, and E(t)=1/H(-t), H(t)=1+g_1*t+...+g_d*t^d, prove [t^m]E(t) >= 0 for every m,d,n.
proof_boundary: Reciprocal-positivity route matrix only; not a proof of Schur positivity, Jensen-window PF-infinity, Jensen hyperbolicity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
required_route_features: proves coefficient nonnegativity of 1/H(-t) for all m
handles H(t)=sum_j binom(d,j) A_{n+j} t^j / A_n for all d,n
does not assume Jensen-window PF-infinity, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0
does not promote finite recurrence stress rows into an all-order theorem
states whether it proves only column shapes or could lift to all Schur/Toeplitz shapes

rows.route_family: finite PF / real-negative-zero endpoint for H(t)
rows.mechanism: If every finite Jensen-window polynomial H(t) has nonnegative coefficients and only real nonpositive zeros, then H(t)=prod_i (1+alpha_i*t) with alpha_i>=0 and 1/H(-t)=prod_i (1-alpha_i*t)^(-1) has nonnegative coefficients.
rows.missing_hypothesis: The real-negative-zero/PF hypothesis is essentially the Jensen-window endpoint for each window and is only finite-checked on the current Sturm/PF grid.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: Using this route as an input would assume the all-window PF conclusion needed by jwpf_06.
rows.next_action: Use this row only as endpoint language and as a consistency check for finite Sturm/PF windows.
rows.proof_boundary: Endpoint consequence only; not an independent bridge theorem.

rows.route_family: positive renewal or convolution representation for the reciprocal coefficients
rows.mechanism: Find a representation of [t^m]1/H(-t) as a sum or integral of nonnegative weights generated from Xi/Phi-specific data, so recurrence positivity follows without checking each m separately.
rows.missing_hypothesis: Missing: no nonnegative renewal kernel, convolution formula, or positive measure representation for the actual binomially weighted Jensen windows is known.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: The raw recurrence has alternating signs in the denominator, so generic coefficient positivity of h_j or adjacent log-concavity is insufficient.
rows.next_action: Search for an Xi/Phi integral identity whose coefficient extraction gives the reciprocal coefficients as manifestly nonnegative quantities.
rows.proof_boundary: Live structural candidate only; the required positive representation is missing.

rows.route_family: standard positive Stieltjes, Jacobi, or S-fraction expansion for 1/H(-t)
rows.mechanism: A standard positive S-fraction or J-fraction would need nonnegative first fraction parameters.
rows.missing_hypothesis: Mismatch: the exact first parameters for E(t)=1/H(-t) are a_2=-g_2/g_1 and lambda_1=-g_2, so positive g_1,g_2 give the wrong sign.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: The reciprocal fraction scout validates the symbolic sign obstruction and checks 735 finite zeta-window rows in the same sign regime.
rows.next_action: Do not cite ordinary positive Stieltjes/Jacobi fraction machinery for E(t); look only for a signed or modified fraction with its own theorem.
rows.proof_boundary: Rejected/mismatch row for the standard positive fraction route; not a proof of or against signed modified fraction machinery.

rows.route_family: companion, production-matrix, or Riordan-array total positivity
rows.mechanism: Encode the recurrence coefficients as entries generated by a nonnegative or totally nonnegative production matrix, so all reciprocal coefficients are path sums.
rows.missing_hypothesis: Missing: the obvious companion matrix for the recurrence has alternating signs, the raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction production matrix has negative path weights, a global length-parity sign cannot repair same-length mixed signs, and the absolute-value sign-state cover overshoots mu_2 by 2*kappa_1; no genuinely modified state-space, Riordan representation, or planar-network production matrix has been found for the actual windows.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: A production matrix for selected low-degree formulas would not be enough, because the degree-3 size-8 and degree-4 size-6 frontier rows reject low-order promotion. The Motzkin obstruction scout validates 735 mu_2 cancellation rows and 630 beta_1 diagonal obstruction rows; the parity-lift obstruction scout validates 5,145 same-length mixed-sign witness rows; and the state-space sign-lift obstruction scout validates 735 absolute-lift gap rows. Together they reject the raw ordinary J-fraction path model, cheap global sign repairs, and the absolute-value sign-state cover as manifest positivity.
rows.next_action: Search for a genuinely modified binomial-transform, state-space doubled, Riordan, or production-matrix representation whose first rows reproduce the column recurrence and whose minors pass the hard frontier checks.
rows.proof_boundary: Live structural candidate only; no total-positive production matrix is currently proved.

rows.mechanism: Kaluza-type results control signs of reciprocal-series coefficients under special log-convexity or related hypotheses.
rows.missing_hypothesis: The available Jensen-window constraints are not the required global hypotheses, and the denominator here is sign-alternating after H(-t).
rows.countermodel_or_gate: The ratio-condition scouts reject nearby generic log-concavity and contraction hypotheses as standalone bridge conditions.
rows.next_action: Do not use Kaluza-type sign language unless a precise theorem is found whose hypotheses match H(-t) and survive the recorded countermodels.
rows.proof_boundary: Mismatch row only; not a proof route.

rows.route_family: generic log-concavity, ratio monotonicity, or finite low-degree positivity
rows.mechanism: Strengthen adjacent log-concavity or ratio conditions until the reciprocal recurrence appears forced.
rows.missing_hypothesis: No tested generic ratio condition is strong enough without becoming tautological or circular.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: Exact positive rational extensions satisfy the candidate ratio constraints while the hard column recurrence rows remain negative.
rows.next_action: Keep these rows as rejection gates for future proposed bridge lemmas.
rows.proof_boundary: Rejected by countermodel; not a proof route.

rows.route_family: finite Arb recurrence stress and finite coverage
rows.mechanism: Check many concrete zeta heat-flow windows with rigorous coefficient enclosures and the recurrence itself.
rows.missing_hypothesis: A finite grid has no all-order mechanism for every degree, shift, lambda, and recurrence size.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: Finite-prefix promotion is explicitly blocked by the countermodel library and dependency graph. The old recurrence stress validates 12,600 positive rows; the extended normalized reciprocal coefficient stress validates 72,600 positive rows across d=2..12, n=0..32, and m=1..40.
rows.next_action: Use the 12,600 recurrence rows and 72,600 extended reciprocal coefficient rows to guide theorem search and falsify bad formulas, not to close the theorem.
rows.proof_boundary: Finite evidence only; not an all-order recurrence theorem.

rows.route_family: lift from column recurrence to all Schur/Toeplitz minors
rows.mechanism: Turn column-shape positivity into all skew-shape Schur positivity through a positive specialization, planar network, or Toeplitz total-positivity theorem.
rows.missing_hypothesis: Column recurrence positivity is necessary but not sufficient for every Schur/Jacobi-Trudi shape; the all-shape positive specialization remains missing.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: The Schur shape contract records many bounded nonzero shapes beyond columns, so a column-only proof cannot close jwpf_06.
rows.next_action: After any reciprocal-positivity theorem is found, test whether it extends to a genuine positive specialization or network for all finite-band shapes.
rows.proof_boundary: Blocked until an independent all-shape lift is proved.

rows.route_family: signed or modified continued fraction compatible with alternating reciprocal signs
rows.mechanism: A signed or modified continued fraction could remain useful if a separate oscillatory, sign-regular, state-space, cancellation, production-matrix, or positive-readout theorem turns its signed Hankel signature, negative ordinary lambda parameters, signed Jacobi beta pattern, ordinary Motzkin cancellation, same-length mixed-sign path obstruction, and oscillatory/resolvent fit constraints into coefficient positivity for 1/H(-t).
rows.missing_hypothesis: Missing: no signed-fraction theorem, exact positive scalar readout, noncircular positive spectral transform, Xi/Phi-specific positive resolvent kernel, or zeta-specific parameter construction has been identified that implies all reciprocal coefficients are nonnegative.
rows.countermodel_or_gate: The standard positive fraction route is blocked at a_2=-g_2/g_1 and lambda_1=-g_2. The signed J-fraction scout validates 3,675 signed reciprocal-Hankel rows and 2,940 signed-lambda rows; the signed Jacobi beta scout validates 3,675 beta rows; the Motzkin obstruction scout validates 735 mu_2 cancellation rows plus 630 beta_1 diagonal obstruction rows; the parity-lift obstruction scout validates 5,145 same-length mixed-sign witness rows; the sign-lift obstruction scout validates 735 absolute-value cover gap rows; the oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix validates 8 fit rows with 0 ready rows; and the positive spectral moment obstruction validates Delta_2=-g_2 plus 735 finite Delta_2<0 rows. These remain finite evidence and rejection gates for the raw ordinary path model, cheap global sign repairs, absolute-value sign-state covers, generic oscillatory spectral language, ordinary positive moment measures, and formal J-fraction existence without coefficient positivity.
rows.next_action: Use the signed J-fraction theorem target, modified signed-model target, oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix, positive-readout theorem target, and positive spectral moment obstruction to search only among surviving state-space, nonordinary positive scalar readout, Xi/Phi positive-kernel, positive-cancellation, or modified production-matrix theorems whose hypotheses match the alternating denominator and survive the hard column recurrence frontier.
rows.proof_boundary: Live structural candidate only; no signed or modified continued-fraction theorem is currently proved.

summary.main_finding: The column recurrence is now reduced to reciprocal coefficient positivity for 1/H(-t).  Real-root/PF endpoint language would imply it but is circular as input; ordinary positive Stieltjes/Jacobi fractions have the wrong first sign; the signed J-fraction and signed Jacobi beta signatures have strong finite support but no all-order theorem; the raw ordinary Motzkin path model, global length-parity sign repair, and absolute-value sign-state cover are blocked by obstruction gates; the oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix, positive-readout theorem target, and positive spectral moment obstruction reject generic entrywise, diagonal-similarity, absolute-value, classical oscillatory, indefinite-language, finite-pattern, endpoint, raw signed-readout, ordinary positive moment-measure, and finite-quadrature shortcuts while leaving only nonordinary positive spectral transform and Xi/Phi positive-kernel variants live conditionally; the modified signed-model target leaves only genuinely modified state-space, modified production/Riordan, oscillatory resolvent, and Xi/Phi positive-cancellation routes live conditionally; the extended reciprocal coefficient stress gives 72,600 finite positive rows but no all-order mechanism; positive renewal, signed or modified continued-fraction, and production-matrix routes are live only if a new Xi/Phi-specific representation is proved; generic ratio conditions and finite grids cannot close the theorem.

invariants: Every live_if_representation_proved row names a missing positive representation.
Every rejected_by_countermodel row cites the ratio/frontier countermodel scouts.
The finite stress grid remains finite_evidence_only.
